hero.subtitle: Shared Socket.IO gateway, JWT handshake auth, and message handlers keyed by type.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The stack under src/common/websocket exposes a single Socket.IO gateway. Features (chat, notifications, etc.) do not create another gateway: they register MessageHandlers that respond to payload.type. The reference module is websocket-example.

points.title: Key ideas
items: JWT required on the connection handshake

architecture.title: Architecture and flow
architecture.description: AppModule imports WebsocketModule.forRoot(), which boots WebsocketGateway and WebsocketService. Each feature module imports WebsocketModule and registers its handlers.

steps.title: Message path
items: Handler validates the DTO and calls the feature service

auth.title: JWT authentication
auth.description: In AbstractWebsocketGateway.afterInit, a Socket.IO middleware requires a token. Without a valid token the connection is rejected.

contract.title: Message contract
contract.description: All feature traffic uses the message event. The type field selects the handler (first-match).

events.title: Events
items: Feature events — you define them (e.g. websocket_example_ack)

serviceApi.title: WebsocketService API
serviceApi.description: The concrete service extends AbstractWebsocketService and is the registration/broadcast entry point.
items: processMessage(client, payload) — first handler with canHandle(type)

createModule.title: Create a WebSocket module
createModule.intro: Follow these steps for a full feature. You do not need a new @WebSocketGateway.

dto.title: 1. DTO
dto.description: Include a stable type and validate with class-validator.

service.title: 3. Service
service.description: Business logic; use client.emit or WebsocketService.sendToClient / broadcast.

module.title: 4. Module + registration
module.description: Import WebsocketModule and register the handler with the REGISTER_HANDLERS factory.

app.title: 5. AppModule
app.description: Ensure WebsocketModule.forRoot() and import your feature module.

multiDto.title: Multiple DTOs in the same module
multiDto.description: Recommended pattern: one DTO and one Handler per type. Register all in the same factory. The example only has one DTO; the pattern below is what real features should use.

example.title: websocket-example module
example.description: Minimal demo: one DTO type example, one handler, in-memory ack, and a REST endpoint for online clients.
items: GET /websocket-example/online — clients active in the last 5s (JWT)

validation.title: Validation
validation.description: Domain validation lives in the handler with class-validator. PayloadValidationPipe (requires type) exists under common/websocket/pipes but is not applied on the gateway today.
items: Optional pipe on the gateway if you want early type checks

client.title: Socket.IO client
client.description: Connect with path /ws and a JWT. Then emit message and listen for feature events.

jwt.title: JWT required
jwt.description: Without a valid handshake token the connection will not start. Get an access token via Auth.

async.title: Async handlers
async.description: processMessage does not await handle(). Async errors do not reject the gateway Promise — handle errors inside the handler.

memory.title: In-memory state
memory.description: The example ack map is per process. Across multiple instances use a shared store (Redis, etc.).

samples.registerComment: // register the handler at module bootstrap
samples.multiRegister: // multiple handlers in the same module
